text:
Once upon a time in a small town nestled among rolling hills, there was a quiet park bordered on three sides by woods. On particularly lazy summer days, the children would gather there, chasing each other around, picking berries, or just lying under the big oak tree, reading magazines.

There was Charlie, a clever little boy with a passion for trains. He had a miniature steam engine tucked away in an old trunk. Every evening he'd dream of journeying across continents, engines chugging triumphantly.

One day, while exploring near the pond, Charlie found something sparkling. It was a lost seashell—a relic from some distant shore. He wondered where it came from. Overcoming shyness, Charlie decided to keep it. He imagined treasures along its sandy ridge.

He'd show it off at school—the most unusual thing anyone had ever found. His parents laughed. "That shell won’t fly," they joked.

But Charlie didn’t care. He wanted to discover secrets. He imagined
